response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "Creating and maintaining a social media account requires vigilance when it comes to dealing with fake information. One must be aware of the potential for false information to spread quickly, especially during election times or when controversial topics are being discussed. Here are some steps you can take to protect yourself from encountering or sharing fake content on social media: 1) Verify information by checking sources and cross-referencing with other credible sources; 2) Be cautious of sensational headlines and articles with little factual evidence; 3) Don't engage in discussions that are divisive or controversial, especially if they have the potential to turn violent. If you do find yourself involved in such conversations, try to remain calm and objective while emphasizing your point of view respectfully; 4) Report any suspicious content immediately to the platform moderators so that it doesn't harm others. It is crucial to be proactive when dealing with fake news because once a rumor starts circulating, it can be challenging to stop it from spreading."
,"reasoning_summary": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must employ critical thinking skills, remain vigilant, and take action quickly. By verifying information through credible sources, being cautious of sensational headlines, avoiding divisive conversations, and reporting suspicious content promptly, individuals can protect themselves from encountering or sharing false information on social media."
,"tone": "informative"

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Creating and maintaining a social media account requires vigilance when it comes to dealing with fake information. One must be aware of the potential for false information to spread quickly, especially during election times or when controversial topics are being discussed. Here are some steps you can take to protect yourself from encountering or sharing fake content on social media: 1) Verify information by checking sources and cross-referencing with other credible sources; 2) Be cautious of sensational headlines and articles with little factual evidence; 3) Don't engage in discussions that are divisive or controversial, especially if they have the potential to turn violent. If you do find yourself involved in such conversations, try to remain calm and objective while emphasizing your point of view respectfully; 4) Report any suspicious content immediately to the platform moderators so that it doesn't harm others. It is crucial to be proactive when dealing with fake news because once a rumor starts circulating, it can be challenging to stop it from spreading.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must employ critical thinking skills, remain vigilant, and take action quickly. By verifying information through credible sources, being cautious of sensational headlines, avoiding divisive conversations, and reporting suspicious content promptly, individuals can protect themselves from encountering or sharing false information on social media.
